title: viola tricolor var. subatlantica
intro: Viola tricolor var. subatlantica, also known as the wild pansy, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the violet family and is native to coastal areas of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and anthocyanins. The health benefits of Viola tricolor var. subatlantica include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve symptoms of digestive issues, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Viola tricolor var. subatlantica include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
